title: Reconnect Process for New Occurrences
text: Reconnect Process for New Occurrences No Database Connection Uniface does the following for all disconnected occurrences with modification status new and no connection to the database: Uniface searches for the occurrence in the component using the disconnected occurrence Id. If the occurrence exists in the component, the reconnect process continues at step 4. If the occurrence does not exist in the component, Uniface recreates the occurrence using the same disconnected occurrence Id. Field data is copied from the disconnected occurrence. Occurrence modification flags are set to New ( $occmod =1, $storetype =1). Field modification flags are set to Modified ( $fieldmod =1). The disconnected occurrence is deleted, and the reconnect process continues with the next disconnected occurrence. With Database Connection Uniface does the following for all disconnected occurrences with modification status new and a connection to the database: Uniface searches for the occurrence in the component using the disconnected occurrence Id. If the occurrence exists in the component, the reconnect process continues at step 4. If the occurrence does not exist in the component, Uniface recreates the occurrence using the same disconnected occurrence Id. Uniface does not search for the occurrence in the database. Field data is copied from the disconnected occurrence. Occurrence modification flags are set to New ( $occmod=1 , $storetype=1 ). Field modification flags are set to Modified ( $fieldmod=1 ). The disconnected occurrence is deleted, and the reconnect process continues with the next disconnected occurrence. Note: After the reconnect process, key validation should be used to check for duplicate keys in the database. Related Links $occmod Return the modification status of an occurrence. $fieldmod Return the modification status of a field. $storetype Return the type of update for the current occurrence.
